channel.title: Cariboo Signals – TTS Preview
channel.description: Azure Neural TTS A/B test feed – temporary, this week only.
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation [Azure TTS]
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [APTN News] The next test for economic reconciliation is explored at Canada 2020 conference and [APTN News] Some people with medical needs are being taken out of Peguis First Nation, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can Indigenous communities deploy their territorial knowledge to build sovereignty rather than create vulnerability? Topics include: Institutional embeddedness vs. co-optation risk in Indigenous-state partnerships, Embodied knowledge transmission through cultural forms as resistance to data extraction, Technology platform ownership and long-term jurisdictional control, Capacity gaps between well-resourced and underfunded Indigenous nations, Digital security and surveillance as extensions of territorial knowledge vulnerabilities.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Denisiqi Services Society

Sources discussed:

  • APTN News: [APTN News] The next test for economic reconciliation is exp...
  • APTN News: [APTN News] Some people with medical needs are being taken o...
  • APTN News: [APTN News] First Nations chiefs call for inquiry into RCMP ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Indoor Spring Rodeo gr...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] The 988 Hotline Is Saving Thousands of Lives,...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] It’s finally happened — renewable energy just ov...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Good news, EV buyers — the average price of elec...
  • APTN News: [APTN News] 5,200-kilometre long-range patrol crosses the fi...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Deportation for cartel-linked ‘hitma...
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] ‘Leah carried a deep love for her family’: Moth...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] The Case of the Experimental Farm Built for 19th-...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] italki is an online language-learning pla...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] LETTER: Thanking West Kelowna reside...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Microsoft unleashes VS Code 1.117 with custom AI mo...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Making RAM at Home in Your Own Semiconductor Fab
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I built the same website using Lovable an...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] 60-sec rooftop cube cabin is a hard-wall mobile ...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] ‘Simply by doing their daily work’: Meta tracks ...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Personalized Vaccine For 'Deadliest Major Can...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] How Gut Bacteria May Affect the Outcome of Cancer...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] BC Must Stop Blaming First Nations for Old-Growth...
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] Tla’amin choreographer sets the dance stage abl...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [APTN News] The next test for economic reconciliation is explored at Canada 2020 conference and [APTN News] Some people with medical needs are being taken out of Peguis First Nation, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can Indigenous communities deploy their territorial knowledge to build sovereignty rather than create vulnerability? Topics include: Institutional embeddedness vs. co-optation risk in Indigenous-state partnerships, Embodied knowledge transmission through cultural forms as resistance to data extraction, Technology platform ownership and long-term jurisdictional control, Capacity gaps between well-resourced and underfunded Indigenous nations, Digital security and surveillance as extensions of territorial knowledge vulnerabilities.

item.title: Community Tech And Governance [Azure TTS]
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] Apple and Google are reportedly pointing users to 'nudify' apps and [Business Insider] The newest prediction markets guru is a middle schooler in braces, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How does opacity in institutional decision-making undermine democratic accountability in rural communities, and what information infrastructure can address it? Topics include: Federal access to information proposals and exemptions for 'transitory' records, TRU Williams Lake campus closure as a governance accountability gap, Irish fuel protests as a case study in civic breakdown from opaque decision-making, Proactive disclosure and shared transparency infrastructure models for rural municipalities, Regional coordination mechanisms through the Cariboo Regional District.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

Sources discussed:

  • Engadget: [Engadget] Apple and Google are reportedly pointing users to...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] The newest prediction markets guru is a m...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake council opposes potent...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Why are fuel price protests sweeping th...

Additional sources provided:

  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Ahead of 2028, Vance Collects Cash, Chits ...
  • The Guardian Global Development: [The Guardian Global Development] Norway’s state telecoms fi...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Quesnel testing Voyent Alert syst...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile Museum project receives grant
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] More Young Men Say Religion Is ‘Very Impor...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Older Women Are in Demand by Younger Men
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Legal migrants remain vulnerable to tra...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Kyiv hit by deadly missile attack amid ...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Got $100 Million? Ukraine Has Just the Fer...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] The Great American GLP-1 Experiment . In the la...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] European Airlines Face Fuel Shortages Within ...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] Help for Medicare Advantage Patients Who Lose...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] Government emails, text messages could be shie...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] Apple and Google are reportedly pointing users to 'nudify' apps and [Business Insider] The newest prediction markets guru is a middle schooler in braces, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How does opacity in institutional decision-making undermine democratic accountability in rural communities, and what information infrastructure can address it? item.title: Working Lands And Industry [Azure TTS]
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] Deezer says AI-made songs make up 44 percent of daily uploads and [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. to opt into part of federal temporary foreign worker expansion, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can electric equipment effectively replace gas-powered tools in Cariboo working landscapes, and who bears the costs and risks of that transition? Topics include: Electric off-road motorcycles (KTM Freeride E) for ranch operations, Total cost of ownership and maintenance differentials between electric and gas equipment, Rural service infrastructure and field-serviceability of electric vehicles, Workforce development and skills transition for electric drivetrain repair, Collective procurement and co-operative models for equipment transition.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Agricultural Research Alliance

Sources discussed:

  • Engadget: [Engadget] Deezer says AI-made songs make up 44 percent of d...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. to opt into part of federal tem...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘You can accomplish anything’: Fello...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake First Nations wins B.C...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Blood Tests Could Reveal Alzheimer's Risk Yea...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] KTM’s flagship electric dual sport is finally st...

Additional sources provided:

  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Chainless recumbent ICE trike scraps the links f...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] RCMP seek information on pickup truc...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] AI Gives 'Problematic' Health Advice Around H...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Valve's new Linux VRAM fix almost triples f...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] AI is already hollowing out the white-collar econom...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] AI is making workers more productive. Their paychec...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] The AI shrinkflation of white-collar jobs: More wor...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] 'White-collar' is too blurry a category to measure ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] AI capital expenditures are booming. Jobs aren't. H...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Your Smartwatch May Be Getting 6 Key Health M...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Six women win 2026 Goldman prize, world...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I ran Cyberpunk 2077 inside a Proxmox VM on...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Sleeping beauty marmot Roger yet to ...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Enclosed ebike uses retractable outriggers to ke...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can electric equipment effectively replace gas-powered tools in Cariboo working landscapes, and who bears the costs and risks of that transition? item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling [Azure TTS]
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. premier’s office says DRIPA changes will not be introduced this spring after all and [100 Mile Free Press] Cancer patient donates saddle sale to 100 Mile House oncology department, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural Cariboo communities build lasting digital storytelling capacity that preserves cultural memory while maintaining sovereignty over their own narratives? Topics include: Digitization infrastructure vs. storytelling capacity building, Cultural sovereignty and governance in digital archiving, Interpretive labor and the role of elders in meaning-making, Technology accessibility for rural communities, Institutional partnerships and power dynamics in cultural preservation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Partners for Literacy

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. premier’s office says DRIPA cha...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Cancer patient donates saddle sale to ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Canada’s alcohol relief ‘step in the...
  • CBC: [CBC] Williams Lake, Quesnel and 100 Mile House firefighters...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] New power for B.C. city’s mayor to r...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Regional district, B.C in stalemate ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Vancouver Island WildFire Whisky pai...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prescribed burns set for north ea...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Benefits of home-ground flour shared a...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Some of the buzziest names in superintell...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Turn shoebox memories into digital keepsakes w...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Surge in demand overwhelms West Koot...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Okanagan Humane Society: An affectio...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. police watchdog probes Quesnel ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Hackaday Podcast Episode 366: DOOM on a Toaster, ...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
